# Task Requirement Model Evidence

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/taskrequirements -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to invariants](../invariants.md)

Executable SSOT: [`task-requirements.riido.json`](task-requirements.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `task-requirements-evidence`.

Proves provider-neutral task required surfaces from executable scheduling behavior, runtime capability projection, and request ingress code.

## Required Surfaces

| Surface | Meaning | Capability flag | Scheduling field | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `structured-event-stream` | stdout/RPC에서 구조화된 event stream을 받을 수 있어야 한다. | `SupportsStructuredEventStream` | `SupportsStreaming` | `surface-structured-constant`, `surface-structured-support` |
| `session-resume` | session/thread resume이 가능해야 한다. | `SupportsResume` | `SupportsResume` | `surface-session-constant`, `surface-session-support` |
| `system-prompt` | system/developer instruction을 native surface로 전달할 수 있어야 한다. | `SupportsSystemPrompt` | `SupportsSystem` | `surface-system-constant`, `surface-system-support` |
| `max-turns` | turn limit을 native surface로 전달할 수 있어야 한다. | `SupportsMaxTurns` | `SupportsMaxTurns` | `surface-max-turns-constant`, `surface-max-turns-support` |
| `mcp` | MCP config/tool bridge를 지원해야 한다. | `SupportsMCP` | `SupportsMCP` | `surface-mcp-constant`, `surface-mcp-support` |
| `tool-hooks` | tool/hook event surface를 지원해야 한다. | `SupportsHookEvents` | `SupportsToolHooks` | `surface-tool-hooks-constant`, `surface-tool-hooks-support` |
| `usage` | token usage metric을 제공해야 한다. | `SupportsUsageMetrics` | `SupportsUsage` | `surface-usage-constant`, `surface-usage-support` |
| `worktree` | daemon이 선택한 task-scoped workdir/worktree를 provider 실행 표면으로 전달할 수 있어야 한다. | `SupportsWorktree` | `SupportsWorktree` | `surface-worktree-constant`, `surface-worktree-support`, `runtimeactor-worktree-missing`, `supervisor-worktree-gate-test` |

## Input Surfaces

- `TaskRequest.RequiredSurfaces`: Go request surface is converted into scheduling.TaskRequirements. Evidence: `task-request-required-surfaces`.
- `metadata.required_surfaces`: legacy/local metadata uses the contracts metadata key vocabulary and is normalized into the same model. Evidence: `metadata-key-ssot`, `metadata-required-surfaces`.

## Local Queue Example

```json
{
  "id": "task-1",
  "provider": "cursor",
  "prompt": "inspect this repo",
  "required_surfaces": ["structured-event-stream", "worktree"],
  "allow_experimental_runtime": true,
  "metadata": {"workspace_id": "ws-1"}
}
```

## Assertions

- required surface names are provider-neutral and must map to a scheduling capability field
- unknown required surfaces fail closed instead of being treated as supported
- reader documentation is generated from this manifest
- worktree support is part of both scheduling eligibility and runtime missing-capability evidence
